name: strube_symbolic_music_generation

A_neutral.description: Minimal conditioning; model generates without explicit Strube, key, or SATB constraints.

deepbach.prompt: random_init=True; no key metadata; no fixed voices; sequence_length_ticks=160; num_iterations=100

coconet.prompt: single C4 seed note at step 0; totalQuantizedSteps=32 by default; no key cadence; numIterations=20 by default

B_key.description: Key-level conditioning toward C major / Baroque context.

deepbach.prompt: C major key metadata constraint; tensor_metadata[:, :, 2] = 8; random_init=True; sequence_length_ticks=160; num_iterations=100

coconet.prompt: single C4 seed note plus final C major cadence chord: C3, G3, C4, E4 during final quarter; totalQuantizedSteps=32 by default; numIterations=20 by default

C_satb.description: SATB-style conditioning using soprano or SATB-style prompt where supported.

deepbach.prompt: fixed soprano voice from first valid Bach chorale seed; generate Alto, Tenor, Bass; voice_index_range=[1, 4]; num_iterations=100

coconet.prompt: fixed soprano notes extracted from first valid Bach chorale seed; infer remaining voices; totalQuantizedSteps=32 by default; numIterations=20 by default

D_full.description: Strongest available conditioning for Bach-style SATB generation.

deepbach.prompt: fixed soprano and bass voices from first valid Bach chorale seed; generate Alto and Tenor; voice_index_range=[1, 3]; num_iterations=100

coconet.prompt: fixed soprano and bass notes extracted from first valid Bach chorale seed; infer Alto and Tenor; totalQuantizedSteps=32 by default; numIterations=20 by default

methodology_note: Prompt consistency design: Each condition (A-D) enforces an equivalent conditioning LEVEL across all three models, not an identical prompt string. DeepBach uses tensor constraints, Coconet uses note seeds, NotaGen uses metadata text tags — these are the native interfaces of each model. Equivalent conditioning level (not identical prompt text) is the correct standard for comparative evaluation in MIR research, consistent with Hadjeres et al. (2017), Huang et al. (2017), and similar papers.
